summary: Joel posts two brief corrections to previous statements. First, he clarifies that his criticism of FORTRAN not allowing functions applied to very old versions, not modern ones, acknowledging he last used WATFOR 27 years prior. Second, he corrects his claim that the Google Mini communicated with Google for PageRank data, noting that Google's product managers confirmed the device deliberately avoids 'phoning home' to protect intranet customers' privacy.
key_points: Old FORTRAN (like WATFOR) had significant limitations that newer versions of the language addressed
The Google Mini was designed to be fully self-contained and does not communicate back to Google, specifically to serve intranet customers
Joel demonstrates willingness to publicly correct factual errors in his writing
money_quotes: They are very religious about NOT phoning home to Google under any circumstances.
key_insight: Public corrections, even minor ones, are worth issuing promptly to maintain credibility and accuracy.
